description: Step into a space where spontaneity meets performance.Improv Level One – From Play to Performance is a 14-hour immersive improv theatre workshop at The Immersion Room, Gurgaon, designed for adults who want to explore creativity, confidence, and live expression.Through a series of interactive theatre games, scene work, and character exploration, participants develop:Spontaneity & quick thinkingPresence & active listeningConfidence in expressionCollaboration & trustStorytelling through performanceThis workshop focuses on learning by doing—breaking inhibitions, embracing uncertainty, and discovering the joy of being fully present.Multi-day Weekend Workshop SeriesMay 3, 2026 | 11:00 AM – 1:00 PMMay 10, 2026 | 11:00 AM – 1:00 PMMay 17, 2026 | 11:00 AM – 1:00 PMMay 24, 2026 | 12:00 PM – 2:00 PMMay 31, 2026 | 11:00 AM – 1:00 PMJune 7, 2026 | 11:00 AM – 1:00 PMJune 14, 2026 | 11:00 AM – 1:00 PMTotal Duration: 14 HoursFormat: In-person | Adults OnlyFinal Showcase:The experience culminates in a live improv performance at The Immersion Room, where participants step onto the stage and perform in front of an audience.

name: Caught in the Middle | Online
description: Often called the “sandwich generation,” this phase of life sits between responsibility and resilience.If you’re in your 40s or 50s, you probably don’t need an explanation, you are living it. Caring for aging parents while still showing up fully for your children. Holding space for everyone else, while trying to keep your own world from unraveling in the process.It is a lot. And it’s often invisible.This is a space to pause, share, and connect with others who understand the weight of it, without needing to explain it. Come talk to people who get it.

name: The Support Club
description: The Support ClubA No Taboo x Scoop Studio experienceWhat if the problem isn’t your body… but the bra you’ve been told fits it?Welcome to The Support Club! An intimate, small-group experience where we unpack everything you thought you knew about bras, sizing, and support… and replace it with something that actually works for your body.Co-hosted by Neelakshi (@plumptopretty and No Taboo Experiences) and Rhea (Bra Fitter and Founder of @shop_scoop_studio), this 90-minute session blends honest conversations, interactive exercises, and a personalized fitting to help you finally understand what real support feels like.Expect:A safe, judgment-free space to talk about bodies, bras, and everything in betweenA guided “bra reality check” to understand what’s actually going on with your current fitSimple, practical tools to decode sizing, support, and comfortA 1:1 personalized fitting by RheaAMA with Neelakshi (confidence to lingerie tips and tricks)A chance to try and shop a curated edit of fuller-bust friendly brasSo, whether you’ve always struggled with fit, constantly adjust your straps, or know something isn’t quite right: this is for you. You don’t leave with just a bra.You leave knowing your body.A quick note on sizingMost people who come to Scoop Studio are wearing the wrong size for their bodies.We’ve seen 36DD become 32G, 38E become 36H, and 34DD become 28H. So many people go from constantly adjusting to finally feeling supported for the first time.This session is designed to help you understand your body and find what truly works for you, not just what you’ve been told your size is.Available sizes: 28-44 Bands | DD-K Cups

name: 1857 Mutiny : A heritage walk in Delhi Ridge
description: 1857 UPRISING & DILLI: (A heritage walk at the Mutiny Memorial & it's neighbourhood in Kamla Nehru Ridge )The year 1857 marked a turning point in Indian history. What began as a sepoy mutiny in Meerut soon transformed into a widespread rebellion against British colonial rule. Within days, the rebelling sepoys marched to Delhi, a city symbolic of imperial power, where they declared the aging Mughal emperor Bahadur Shah Zafar as the leader of the uprising.The Mutiny Memorial, now known as Ajitgarh, is a prominent monument located on the Delhi Ridge, near Kashmere Gate. It was constructed in 1863 by the British to honor their soldiers and officials who died during the 1857 uprising.Let’s explore the stories of the 1857 uprising and its impacts, at the Mutiny Memorial (a haunted site ) & its neighborhood landmarks (Ashokan Pillar, Baoli, Pir Ghaib Hunting Lodge & the Hindu Rao Hospital)9th May, 7.30am onwardsmeeting point: Bara Hindurao Hospital entry gate

description: Sodhilecious began as a simple intention—feeding our own families and children food made with honesty.This base kitchen visit is an open invitation to experience that same honesty firsthand. Step inside and see exactly how your cookies and breads are made—from sourcing to preparation to packaging. Nothing is hidden.You’ll walk through our processes, understand our standards, and meet the people behind every product.No shortcuts. No compromises. Just clean, consistent, transparent baking.Because loyalty isn’t built through marketing—it’s built when you truly know what goes into what you eat.Come see it for yourself.

name: The Poetics of Time: A Poetic Immersion into Visual Arts
description: Dillipoetry, in collaboration with Anant Art Gallery, invites you to The Poetics of Time, a one-of-a-kind interactive poetry workshop where poetry and visual art​ coalesce into a shared experience. As one artist observed, “art is not a history of grand​ masterpieces; it is a record of human attempts.” This holds equally true for poetry, the​ experience is yet another attempt to bring the two into a​n embodied dialogue.The workshop anchors itself in the themes of reverie, pause, and rupture—how​ they unfold in​ one’s inner life and in the world around us, personally,​ systemically and politically.​ Conceived as a response to the ongoing exhibition​ Reverie. Pause. Rupture, the workshop​ engages with the exhibition through​ poetry. The group show, featuring leading contemporary​ artists, “challenges the​ artist and viewer alike to express what may be inexpressible. It reflects​ on time,​ its apparent continuity, as well as the sudden breaks that give rise to new​ beginnings,”​ notes curator Gayatri Sinha.The two-hour-long workshop with its reflective prompts and guided exercises​ promises to bring​ an absurd interplay of visual, somatic and creative mediums to help us move through the idea of​ personal imagination — with prescribed​ writings written across a wide timeline by some of the​ wise voices like Gaston Bachelard, Audre Lorde, Mary Oliver, Naomi Shihab Nye among others.About the host: Rachit is a poet and founder of Dillipoetry—a literary platform known for its thematic, experimental poetry walks, workshops, and training programmes. He is the former Creative Head at Youth Alliance, where he designed and facilitated transformational leadership journeys for young social leaders across India. With over a decade in the social development sector, his work spans diverse social issues. His poetry has appeared in 25+ literary platforms and anthologies. His recent series Memories of Yami and The Asthma City—tracing the Yamuna River through Delhi and the city’s escalating air crisis—were published by Down To Earth magazine.Rachit has been selected for international residencies, including Language is a Queer Thing, a programme by The Queer Muslim Project in collaboration with the British Council, UK. He received the Germinator Grant by Ecoversity for Dillipoetry’s regenerative approach to arts and community.He has performed twice at BBC Contains Strong Language—the UK’s largest poetry festival (Leeds 2023, Bradford 2025)—and at institutions including the French Institute India, Goethe-Institut, British Council, and Alliance Française. His work has appeared on BBC Radio 3’s The Verb. He also served as Lead Poet and Advisor to O.P. Jindal Global University’s programme, We Are Meant to Sing as One.

No moderation. No clean takeaways. Just people presenting things they probably shouldn’t have spent this much time thinking about. name: Bagh-e-Jud: The Lodhi Garden Heritage Walk
description: This Sunday, visit Lodhi Garden to explore the Delhi Sultanate's last major necropolis. We will delve into the stories of the Sayyids and Lodhis through their monuments, including the tombs of Muhammad Shah and Sikandar Lodi, as well as Bara Gumbad and Shisha Gumbad. These sites represent a pivotal period in Delhi’s history, just before the Mughal influx, when architecture, dynasties, and the city itself were evolving. Covering 90 acres, Lodhi Garden today appears as a tranquil urban park, yet it conceals one of the most significant archaeological remains from the late Delhi Sultanate era. Since very little architecture from the Sayyid and Lodhi periods still exists in Delhi, this site offers a vital glimpse into that era. Join us for an early morning walk through history, architecture, and forgotten dynasties, exploring the many subtle layers of Delhi still quietly preserved within the gardens.

location.address: Lodhi Rd, Lodhi Gardens, Lodhi Estate, New Delhi, Delhi 110003, India

name: Chandini Chowk – Spiritual and Living Heritage
description: Chandni Chowk is not just a marketplace; it is a living museum where the 17th-century Mughal urban design breathes alongside the chaos of modern commerce.Designed in 1650 by Jahanara Begum, emperor Shah Jahan’s daughter, Chandni Chowk was once a grand canal-side boulevard. The water from Faiz Nahar flowed through its centre, reflecting the moonlight and giving the street its name. Today, the canal is gone, but the flow remains in the form of a relentless human tide.We will wander the interfaith mile, a single stretch of road where major places of worship for different faiths have coexisted for centuries.Join Mera शहर — My City Walks for a morning of storytelling as we navigate our way through the Kutcha’s and Katra’s of Chandini Chowk. This isn't just a walk through a marketplace; it’s a journey through a 375-year-old living museum where every turn of a lane reveals a layer of Delhi’s complex identity.

location.address: Chandni Chowk, Delhi, India

name: Old Cinema Halls of Purani Dilli-A  heritage walk
description: Old Cinema halls of Shahjahanabad (Old Delhi) heritage walkBefore multiplexes and OTT, there were single-screen cinema halls—temples of Bollywood where whistles echoed, stars arrived for premieres, and cinema was a shared emotion.Join us for a heritage walk through Chandni Chowk (Purani Delhi) as we revisit the now-shut, iconic cinema halls that shaped India’s love affair with films since Independence.Each hall had its own personality—its loyal audience, favourite production houses, and directors who swore by it.We’ll explore stories of legendary screenings, star visits, and how many of these halls began as theatre spaces, later transforming into talkies when sound entered cinema. Today, most stand silent and anonymous—but their stories are anything but.Cinema halls we’ll trace:Excelsior, New Amar, Moti, Majestic & Kumar Talkies .Date : 10th May , Sunday8.30 am onwards📍 Meeting Point: Chawri Bazaar Metro Station, Gate No. 1 (on metro yellow line)🕰 Duration: ~2 to 2.5 hours🚶 Route: Chawri Bazaar & Chandni ChowkCome walk with us into a bygone era of velvet seats, hand-painted posters, and the magic of Bollywood—when cinema wasn’t just watched, it was felt.No parking available at the meeting point.

name: Echoes of Empire: A Walk Through Qutub Complex
description: Step into a living museum of ambition and power at the Qutub Minar Complex, a UNESCO World Heritage Site that defines the Delhi skyline. This tour takes you through centuries of history, beginning with the soaring Qutub Minar itself—an 800-year-old victory tower of intricately carved sandstone and marble.Discover the fascinating transition of empires as we explore the Quwwat-ul-Islam Mosque, featuring some of the earliest Islamic architecture in India. Stand in awe of the Iron Pillar, a 1,600-year-old metallurgical wonder that remains miraculously rust-free. We’ll also visit the serene Tomb of Iltutmish, the grand Alai Darwaza gateway, and the massive, unfinished Alai Minar—a monument to Alauddin Khalji’s bold, unfulfilled dreams.Join us to uncover the legends etched into these red sandstone walls. Please note that the fee is not for access to the site, but rather for the curation of this experience.Contact Us: +91 9041409952Eamil:  uttardharohar@gmail.com

location.address: Qutub MInar Ticket Counter, Seth Sarai, Mehrauli, New Delhi, Delhi 110030, India

name: The Body Keeps Score?
description: Join us for a session that pulls back the curtain on the hidden logic behind our most challenging moments, arguments that escalate out of nowhere and the silences that stretch too long. About the LectureThat fight that erupted over nothing, the moment you said something you immediately regretted, or the "freeze" that swallowed your voice; none of it is random. Your mind and body are in a constant, high-stakes conversation, and most of the time, that conversation is running the show without your permission.When stress hits, your mind-body connection does more than just affect your mood; it rewires how you listen, how you think, and how you show up for the people you love. What lives in your body inevitably shapes what happens between people. In this session, we unpack the neuroscience of our reactions to understand why we explode, why we go cold, and how our biological wiring plays out in our most intimate and everyday relationships.The session explores:The Hidden Conversation: How the mind and body react together to drive our social interactions.The Anatomy of a Fight: Why small disagreements escalate into major conflicts based on internal "scoring."Fight, Flight, or Freeze: The biological reasons why some people react with anger while others withdraw.Rewiring for Connection: How to recognize the physical triggers that lead to relationship "costs" and how to manage them.Flow of the Evening4:00 PM – Doors open: Come in, find your seat, and get comfortable.4:05 – 4:20 PM – Place your orders and start a few conversations.4:30 PM – The learning begins.5:15 PM – Open Q&A and discussion with Dr. Sahni.5:30 PM onwards – Stay a while: More eating, more drinking, more conversations.About the SpeakerProf. (Dr.) Sanjeev P. Sahni is a distinguished psychologist and academic leader with over 35 years of experience spanning the globe. He is the Founder and Principal Director of the Jindal Institute of Behavioural Sciences and the President of the Community Psychology Association of India (CPAI).A former Visiting Scientist at the University of Houston and University of Chicago, Dr. Sahni’s expertise covers a vast landscape, from Criminal and Cyber Psychology to Neuropsychology and Sports Performance. He has authored over 23 books and trained more than 300,000 educators and students worldwide. His work is dedicated to integrating behavioural science into the fabric of education and policy, empowering individuals to move beyond their "excuses" toward true performance excellence.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from St. Stephen’s, Delhi who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

location.address: Siyah Arthouse, 3rd Floor 261, Plot 8, Westend Marg, Saidulajab, Saket, New Delhi, Delhi 110030, India

name: Identity in Transition: A Psycho-Social Analysis
description: Join us for a lecture that dives into a transformation happening in our pockets and on our screens, one that is reshaping who we are and how we feel. About the LectureOur identity used to be shaped by our face-to-face relationships and physical communities. Today, much of that construction has moved online. But what happens to our mental health when our "self" is curated for an audience?This lecture explores the dynamic relationship between digital identity and psychological well-being. Moving beyond simple critiques of screen time, it examines the core dimensions of the self - personal, social, and collective, and how they shift in a digital landscape.The session explores:The Architecture of the Digital Self. How social media allows for identity experimentation and curated self-presentation.The psychological mechanics of social comparison, FOMO, and the quest for external validation through likes and comments.Understanding the tension and anxiety that arise from discrepancies between our online and offline selves.How these platforms offer vital spaces for social support, belonging, and mental health awareness, the digital silver lining.As we navigate an increasingly hybrid world, this session invites us to consider how digital literacy and mindful engagement can help us protect our well-being and foster a more authentic sense of self.About the SpeakerDr. Poonam Yadav is a psychologist and Assistant Professor at Gargi College, University of Delhi. With an M.Phil. and PhD from Jawaharlal Nehru University, her expertise spans social psychology, media psychology, and organizational behavior.Dr. Yadav has taught extensively on research methodology, personality development, and health and well-being. She is a published researcher who has presented her work at both national and international levels, bringing a deep academic rigor to the study of how modern media shapes the human mind. Beyond her academic role, she holds a Diploma in Guidance and Counselling, bridging the gap between theoretical research and applied psychological support.All Grounds for Thought tickets include a ₹250 coupon redeemable on food and beverages at the venue.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

You can bring your biological mom or anyone who is like your mom. Be it your maasi, daadi, mother-in-law, bua, that older sister, chachi, badi maa. Boys are allowed too, but only with your mom :)If your mom is in the town with you, this is the best Mother's Day gift.If you are a mom, this is the me time you need.If your mom is not around, you should still celebrate the day with SWAG and other amazing moms...Can't wait to see you there!

If you have caught yourself going numb just to cope. We see you.All Living Things Environmental Film Festival is back with the third season of Film Club. A monthly meet up across 12 cities over 5 months, orbiting environmental cinema that cracks you open without trapping you in doom and gloom.It is a room full of people choosing to show up in cities whose infrastructure persuades you to stay home, choosing to sit together, watch together, and turn private anxiety into collective imagination. Messy. Human. Honest conversation. With snacks. Always snacks.

name: Before You Blame
description: Before You Blame: A Workshop on Navigating Relationships with ClarityOverviewMost relationship conflicts are not just about what happened; they are about how we interpret what happened. This session focuses on exploring how our thoughts shape our connections and why friction often feels more intense than the situation warrants. "Before You Blame" is a reflective workshop designed to help you look at your assumptions and inner patterns, moving you from reactivity toward steadiness and emotional responsibility.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community-led initiative dedicated to creating safe and mindful spaces. Through intimate workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities, from mental well-being to personal growth, with empathy and shared human connection.What to ExpectPattern Recognition: Explore how common emotional triggers and ego-driven patterns shape conflicts in marriage, family, or work.Internal Focus: Instead of trying to fix others, we gently shift the focus inward to understand our own interpretations and responses.Practical Tools: Learn actionable ways to question stressful thoughts and practice perspective shifts using real-life scenarios.Safe Exploration: A supportive, non-judgmental environment for honest discussion. This is a space for learning and reflection, not a lecture or therapy.Key TakeawaysClarity: Gain a deeper understanding of recurring relationship dynamics.Responsiveness: Walk away with tools to help you respond thoughtfully instead of reacting impulsively.Emotional Ownership: Build the internal steadiness needed to navigate life without requiring others to change first.Details🗓️ Date: 16th May (Sunday)⏰ Time: 11:00 AM📍 Location: Kanchanjunga Towers, Sector 56🎟️ Fee: ₹900 (Introductory offer: ₹200 off original price of ₹1100)

name: How Does Music Bring Us Joy?
description: Join us for a session that dismantles the idea of music as just entertainment and reveals it as a biological and evolutionary dialogue written into our very DNA. About the LectureWe have all experienced it: the sudden urge to tap a foot, the involuntary head bang, or the "chills" that run down our spine during a crescendo. But why should a specific arrangement of sounds trigger such powerful physical and emotional reactions?This session takes us on a cross-disciplinary round trip through Mathematics, Physiology, and Evolutionary Biology to answer a deceptively simple question: Why does music bring us joy? By understanding the biological machinery that allows us to engage with sound, we gain a deeper way of knowing ourselves, for music represents much of what it means to be human.The session explores:The Evolutionary Context: Comparing the role of music in birds and humans to understand its origins.The Auditory Gateway: How our hearing and brain processing transform vibrations into meaning.The Mathematics of Sound: Decoding the unique patterns and structures that make music distinct from noise.The Survival of the Grooviest: Examining the selective pressures that made music an essential trait for our species.Flow of the Evening4:00 PM – Doors open: Come in, find your seat, and get comfortable.4:05 – 4:20 PM: Place your orders and start a few conversations.4:30 PM: The learning begins.5:15 PM: Open Q&A and discussion with Tejas.5:30 PM onwards – Stay a while: More eating, more drinking, and more conversations.About the SpeakerTejas Jaishankar is the Co-Founder and "Resident Nerd" at Upstairs with Us. A seasoned metal guitarist, Tejas occupies the unique space of a listener who deeply admires Indian Classical music but approaches it from the outside, a journey that inspired his mission to make the genre accessible to everyone.With over 10 years of experience as a fitness entrepreneur and educator, Tejas has founded several startups including IIFEM and Shapemenow Technologies. He specializes in building businesses that bridge the gap between complex science and everyday people. When he isn’t deconstructing musical theory, he can be found pursuing eclectic "sidequests" like re-creating prehistoric stone tools.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: The Algorithm as Therapist
description: Join us for a lecture that explores a subtle shift in our inner lives: the moment we stopped turning to people for understanding and started turning to code. About the LectureA "good" therapy session is often messy. It involves sitting with ambiguity, tracing complex relationships, and allowing contradictions to unfold. But we now live in a "Rapido" world that prizes speed and efficiency. Today, AI chatbots offer instant validation, neat summaries, and structured ways of processing our emotions.For many, these tools serve the "missing middle," providing support for those who need more than a wellness app but cannot access formal care. However, as we increasingly lean on algorithms, we begin to privilege the parts of ourselves that are rational and productive. Emotional messiness starts to feel like a "bug" to be fixed rather than a human experience to be understood.The session explores:How AI is filling the gap in mental health care and what it offers the modern user.Why turning to algorithms encourages us to be coherent and productive at the expense of our complex, "messy" selves.Which ways of understanding our pain are being amplified by technology, and which are being lost?What it means to maintain psychological depth in an age of automated empathy.At its core, this talk asks: as the algorithm becomes our therapist, what kind of selves are we being encouraged to become?About the SpeakerAnnie Baxi is a mental health practitioner, qualitative researcher, and Visiting Professor at Ashoka University and BML University. Her work focuses on critical and cultural approaches to psychology, questioning mainstream notions of "normalcy," health, and wellness.With a background that bridges academic rigor and clinical practice, Annie explores how culture and technology intersect with our well-being. Beyond her professional work, she is a devoted pet parent and a collector of picture storybooks, believing that some of the most profound human truths are often found in the simplest forms.Ticket IncludesAll Grounds for Thought tickets include a ₹250 coupon redeemable on food and beverages at the venue.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Luxury Uncovered: The Hidden Stories Behind Brands
description: Join us for a session that pulls back the curtain on the world of luxury, moving beyond the logos and price tags to reveal the messy, accidental, and fascinating realities that build iconic brands. About the LectureLuxury is often presented to us as a narrative of effortless perfection and heritage. But beneath the polished surface lies a deeper reality shaped by constraints, cultural shifts, and strategic improvisations that are rarely discussed in public.This session explores the "unseen" side of some of the world's most famous fashion houses. From chance encounters that birthed legendary products to functional innovations that were engineered into symbols of aspiration, we will uncover how luxury isn't just about what is made, but how the mythology around it is constructed. The session explores:The Architecture of Myth: How brands transform accidental origins into timeless stories.Engineered Scarcity: The strategic intent behind making products difficult to find.The Improvisation Factor: How constraints and unexpected inspirations lead to iconic creations.The Intersection of Creative & Commerce: How luxury continues to evolve through an interplay of culture and storytelling.Whether you are a creative professional or simply curious about the business of desire, this talk offers a fresh lens into the forces that truly shape the modern luxury landscape.Flow of the Evening4:00 PM – Doors open: Come in, find your seat, and get comfortable.4:05 – 4:20 PM: Place your orders and start a few conversations.4:30 PM: The learning begins.5:15 PM: Open Q&A and discussion with Divam.5:30 PM onwards – Stay a while: More eating, more drinking, and more conversations.About the SpeakerDivam Jain is a Division Leader at Pearl Academy School of Business, where he leads programs at the intersection of fashion, luxury, and the creative industries. Educated both in India and abroad, he brings a global perspective to creative business education, blending diverse cultural insights with modern industry practices.Divam's work focuses on reimagining luxury education by exploring consumer psychology, storytelling, and the impact of AI on branding. Known for his insight-driven sessions, he has led immersive collaborations with global brands, helping students and professionals navigate the real-world challenges of the business of creativity.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Her Interest by Zerodha - Delhi
description: Women have a complicated relationship with money. And it starts early. Many families shield their daughters from anything related to ‘finance’, but this well-meaning protection ends up being a disservice. For when these young women step into the real world, they lack the confidence to make financial decisions - not because they’re incapable, but because they were never given information on budgeting, savings, and investments. Later in life, many continue to rely on their partners for investment decisions. This dependency on others will only stop when they have the right information and toolkit to take back control. That’s where we come in.We launch 'In Her Interest' on 22nd May in Delhi and will focus on one core idea for the launch:Building financial security for women.The session will be led by two amazing experts who truly get it.Zeba Zaidi Adeeb, Co-Founder - Spectacom Global, Creator of Devils Circuit & The Yoddha RaceManisha Pande, Tax Partner, Aeka Advisors (ex - GE, AA/EY)This session is on  Finance and the Entrepreneur.Once you buy a ticket, you also get to send a question that the speakers will address during the session.Note: Each session is theme-based, this session is about 'Finance and the Entrepreneur' - we are not talking stocks and shares.

name: When Power Claims Victimhood
description: Join us for an evening of unpacking how the language of equality is being reshaped in India’s courts, and what it means when power begins to claim victimhood.About the LectureWe often think of equality as a fixed principle, clear, neutral, and universally agreed upon. But what if the definition of equality itself is being redefined in real time?Drawing from landmark cases and ongoing constitutional debates, this lecture explores the idea of equality under the Indian Constitution and how it is being contested today. At its core is a provocative question, what happens when historically dominant groups begin to frame themselves as victims?Through a close reading of recent litigation, the session examines how legal arguments are reframing structural advantage as disadvantage, and how courts are becoming arenas where these competing narratives are negotiated.Moving across themes of caste, religion, gender, and institutional power, the lecture explores:Power and Victimhood: How dominant groups are increasingly adopting the language of historical injustice and reparationsEquality as Transformation: Why the Constitution envisioned equality not as neutrality, but as a tool to address deep-rooted structural inequalitiesThe Courtroom as Battleground: How contemporary legal disputes, from reservations to religious rights, are reshaping constitutional meaningReverse Discrimination Debates: The rise of arguments that position corrective measures as unfair to historically privileged groupsWhat’s at Stake: How these shifts could redefine the very foundation of India’s constitutional projectFlow of the Evening5:00 PM: Doors open at Social, Saket: Come in, find your seat, and get comfortable5:00 – 5:20 PM: Place your orders and start a few conversations5:30 PM: The learning begins6:15 PM: Open Q&A and discussion with Ms. Wadekar6:30 PM onwards: Stay a while: More eating, more drinking, more conversationsAbout the SpeakerMs. Disha Wadekar is a constitutional lawyer with over a decade of litigation experience in India. An LL.M. graduate from Columbia Law School and a Fulbright scholar, her work sits at the intersection of law, social justice, and structural inequality.She has been involved in several significant constitutional cases, including the Sabarimala temple entry case and the EWS reservation case. She has represented the families of Rohith Vemula and Payal Tadvi in their fight against institutional discrimination, as well as journalist Sukanya Shantha in a petition challenging caste-based division of labour in Indian prisons. In 2018, she was the only woman lawyer representing Dalit victims before the Bhima Koregaon Judicial Inquiry Commission.In 2022, she was appointed Additional Special Public Prosecutor in a case concerning the rape of a Dalit girl in Rajasthan.Beyond litigation, Ms. Wadekar has taught Constitutional Law and Critical Legal Studies at National Law University, Delhi and O.P. Jindal Global University. She is also the co-founder of CEDE (Community for the Eradication of Discrimination in Education and Employment), an organisation working to increase representation of marginalised communities in the legal profession and judiciary.Her work extends to legal research and consultancy with institutions such as the Centre for Women’s Development Studies and Project 39A, and her writing has been featured in leading journals and platforms.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: The Listening Room
description: The Listening Room: A Space for Deep ListeningOverviewIn a world that is always talking, we rarely get the chance to truly listen. The Listening Room is a unique session dedicated to the art of being present. It is a space to share, to hear others, and to practice the kind of attention that builds real connection and empathy.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community led initiative dedicated to creating safe and mindful spaces. Through intimate workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities from mental well being to personal growth with empathy and shared human connection.What to expectActive Listening: Practice being fully present while others speak without the need to interrupt or give immediate advice.Shared Stories: A safe environment to share your own thoughts or experiences and feel truly heard by a small group.Quiet Reflection: Moments of silence to process what has been shared and to notice how deep listening changes our perspective.Supportive Connection: A morning at Greenr focused on human presence and the simple power of witnessing each other’s stories.Details🗓️ Date: 19th April (Sunday)⏰ Time: 11:00 AM📍 Location: Greenr, Golf Course Ext Road🎟️ Contribution: ₹700

name: The Politics of Motherhood
description: Join us on Sunday, May 24, for a conversation that asks a question we’ve long internalised but rarely examined closely: What if the way we talk about mothers isn’t just cultural, but deeply political?About the LectureMotherhood is often framed as instinctive, natural, and deeply personal. But beneath these ideas lies a powerful and persistent narrative, one that places the burden of blame, responsibility, and perfection squarely on mothers.This session explores how motherhood has been shaped by cultural expectations that are often contradictory and impossible to meet. A mother is told she loves too much or too little, is too present or not present enough. Working mothers are scrutinised, while those who stay at home are diminished. Even the choice to not become a mother carries its own weight of judgment.At its core, this lecture reframes these experiences, not as individual failings, but as part of a larger system.The session explores:The Blame Narrative: How mothers have historically been positioned as responsible for outcomes beyond their controlCultural Contradictions: The impossible standards placed on women across different forms of motherhoodLanguage as Power: How psychological and cultural terms, from ‘mother wound’ to attachment styles, can unintentionally reinforce blameInternalised Shame: How these narratives shape the way mothers see themselves, and each otherRewriting the Story: What it means to view motherhood through a lens of context, care, and social structures rather than individual faultAbout the SpeakerDr. Shelja Sen is a Narrative & Family Therapist, writer, and Co-founder of Children First. She is a TED speaker, a columnist with The Indian Express, and the author of three critically acclaimed books: All You Need is Love, Imagine, and Reclaim Your Life.She serves as a Clinical Tutor at the University of Melbourne and is the Editor-in-Chief of the International Journal of Narrative Therapy & Community Work. Her work is rooted in building culturally aligned, ethical practices that bring together an intersectional lens, social justice, and innovation.At heart, she is a storyteller, someone deeply invested in how the stories we inherit shape the lives we live.All Grounds for Thought tickets include a ₹250 coupon redeemable on food and beverages at the venue.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Why We Taste With Our Ears
description: This lecture promises to be an evening that rethinks something as simple as eating, by tuning to a sense we rarely notice at the table.About the LectureWhy does the sound of rain make fried food more irresistible? Why does music change how we experience a glass of wine?This session begins with a simple but surprising idea: taste doesn’t start in the mouth, it often begins in the ear.Drawing on extensive research by Bruite Magazine across the subcontinent, this lecture explores how sound quietly shapes the way we anticipate, experience, and remember food. From the rhythm of a bustling street to the curated playlists of a fine-dining restaurant, auditory cues influence everything from perceived texture to indulgence and value.Moving across everyday eating environments and contemporary food spaces, the session explores:The Sound of Anticipation: How auditory cues shape cravings even before the first biteSonic Texture: Why crunch, sizzle, and silence can alter how we perceive flavour and freshnessAtmosphere & Memory: How music, noise, and rhythm influence mood and the way meals stay with usDesigning Food Experiences: The role of sound in restaurants, bars, supper clubs, and immersive dining formatsBlending cultural observation, field research, and sharp storytelling, this session offers a fresh lens on something deeply familiar.Flow of the Evening5:00 PM: Doors open at Siyah Arthouse, Saket: Come in, find your seat, and get comfortable5:00 – 5:20 PM: Place your orders and start a few conversations5:30 PM: The learning begins6:15 PM: Open Q&A and discussion with Ms. Ramya6:30 PM onwards: Stay a while: More eating, more drinking, more conversationsAbout the SpeakerRamya G Sri is the Co-founder and Editor-in-Chief of Bruite Magazine, one of South Asia’s leading independent food and culture publications known for its deeply researched, original storytelling and on-ground reporting.Through Bruite, she collaborates with chefs, historians, artisans, and communities across the subcontinent to explore how food intersects with culture, memory, and perception—often through unexpected and underexplored lenses.She also co-leads Bruite Media Co., a specialised brand advisory that has worked with over 500 brands and founders across F&B, lifestyle, D2C, and consumer tech. Her work spans brand launches, communication strategy, and creative training—shaping how brands tell stories and connect with audiences.Her contributions to storytelling have been recognised with the Laadli Media Award for Gender-Sensitive Storytelling and the Abby Silver Award for Advertising. Across her work, she brings together rigorous research, editorial depth, and a strong instinct for how narrative shapes both culture and consumer experience.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Improv Comedy Show: Play2Perform Level 2
description: After 14 hours of high-energy, no-holds-barred workshopping, this ensemble—now working at Level 2—steps into performance with sharper instincts, bolder choices, and a deepened sense of play. What unfolds is completely unscripted, wildly unpredictable, and built in the momenThis isn’t just a show you watch. It’s one you fuel.Your suggestions spark scenes. Your ideas twist the story. Your laughter drives the rhythm.A single word can spiral into a love story, a disaster, or something beautifully absurd. One performer holds the thread, the rest run with it—and somehow, chaos turns into magic.No scripts. No second takes. Just quick thinking, deep listening, and fearless fun.Expect moments that land perfectly, moments that go hilariously sideways, and everything in between. That’s where the real joy lives.HighlightsFully improvised, unscripted comedyAudience participation shapes the showPerformed by Level 2 trained ensembleHigh-energy, interactive experienceEvery show is completely uniqueAbout the HostAnshu DagaAn improv facilitator and performer guiding immersive, high-energy workshops that focus on spontaneity, presence, and creative expression.Come for the spontaneity. Stay for the surprises.Don’t plan the night—just secure your seat!
